birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It can be found in the southern Atlantic Ocean, the southern Indian Ocean, and the Southern Ocean

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Antarctic petrel, as its name implies, lives and breeds in the Southern Ocean, and on the Antarctic islands. They nest on snow-free cliffs and rock faces, on the coast or on offshore islands. However, they have been found up to 250 km inland. Another common roosting spot is icebergs. Breeding colonies during the October–November breeding period, can be as large as 200,000 pairs. However, the largest single colony ever observed, Mühlig-Hofmann Mountains, was estimated to be about one million Antartic petrels, presumably including non-breeders and chicks. They occasionally migrate to Australia or New Zealand in late winter. Unfortunately, this tends to occur when they're caught in a bad storm.{{Cite web|last=Miskelly|first=C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The great skua (Stercorarius skua), sometimes known by the name bonxie in Britain, is a large seabird in the skua family Stercorariidae
